inputLabel: Choose the correct code to get the length:

questions.html: Which correctly gets the length of a list called items?
questions.notice: len(items) — in Python, len() is a function you pass the list into

questions.html: Which correctly gets the length of a list called scores?
questions.notice: len(scores) — len() works with any list, just pass the list name in

questions.html: Which correctly prints the length of a list called names?
questions.notice: print(len(names)) — wrap len() inside print() to display the result

questions.html: Which correctly prints the length of a list called colours?
questions.notice: print(len(colours)) — len() goes inside print(), not the other way around

questions.html: Which correctly stores the length of a list called fruits in a variable?
questions.notice: total = len(fruits) — you can store the result of len() just like any other value

questions.html: Which correctly gets the length of a list called players?
questions.notice: len(players) — Python uses len(), not .size() or .length like other languages

questions.html: Which correctly prints the length of a list called pets?
questions.notice: print(len(pets)) — len() is the only correct way to get a list's length in Python

questions.html: Which correctly stores the length of a list called cities in a variable called total?
questions.notice: total = len(cities) — assign the result of len() to the variable

questions.html: Which correctly gets the length of a list called numbers?
questions.notice: len(numbers) — len() is a built-in Python function, pass the list as an argument

questions.html: Which correctly prints the length of a list called days?
questions.notice: print(len(days)) — you can't call .print() on a number, print() is a function

questions.html: Which correctly stores the length of a list called words in a variable called count?
questions.notice: count = len(words) — the variable goes on the left, the value on the right

questions.html: Which correctly gets the length of a list called ages?
questions.notice: len(ages) — Python does not have .length, .size() or sizeof() for lists
